GRID_LUA_FNC_G_LED_ADDRESS_GET_usage: led_address_get(int element, int n) Returns the hardware index of an element's n-th led, or nil if either parameter is out of bounds.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_MIDI_SEND_usage: midi_send(int channel, int command, int parameter1, int parameter2) Sends standard MIDI message
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_IMMEDIATE_SEND_usage: immediate_send(int x, int y, string lua_code) Executes lua_code on the module addressed by x and y. Use x = nil and y = nil to trigger on all modules.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_ELEMENT_COUNT_usage: element_count(void) Returns the number of elements on the current module.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_CALIBRATION_RESET_usage: calibration_reset() Deletes the calibration file and initializes all calibrations to their defaults.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_POTMETER_CALIBRATION_GET_: potmeter_calibration_get() Returns raw potentiometer values as an array of integers.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_POTMETER_CENTER_SET_usage: potmeter_center_set({ int c1, ... }) Sets potentiometer calibration centers from an array of integers.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_POTMETER_DETENT_SET_usage: potmeter_detent_set({ int c1, ... }, bool high) Sets potentiometer detent bounds from an array of integers, where high = true sets high bounds and high = false sets low bounds.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_RANGE_CALIBRATION_GET_usa: range_calibration_get() Returns raw minimum and maximum values as an array of integer arrays.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_RANGE_CALIBRATION_SET_usa: range_calibration_set({ { int min1, int max1 }, ... }) Sets minimum and maximum values from an array of integer arrays.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_DRAW_SWAP_usage: grid_gui_draw_swap(screen_index) Updates the screen with the contents of the background buffer.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_DRAW_PIXEL_usage: grid_gui_draw_pixel(screen_index, x, y, {r, g, b}) Draws a pixel at (x, y) with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_DRAW_LINE_usage: grid_gui_draw_line(screen_index, x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Draws a line between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_DRAW_RECTANGLE_usage: grid_gui_draw_rectangle(screen_index, x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Draws a rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_DRAW_RECTANGLE_FILLED: grid_gui_draw_rectangle_filled(screen_index, x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Draws a filled rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_DRAW_RECTANGLE_ROUNDE: grid_gui_draw_rectangle_rounded(screen_index, x1, y1, x2, y2, radius, {r, g, b}) Draws a rounded rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points using pixel based radius with the specified 8-bit color channels.

grid_gui_draw_rectangle_rounded_filled(screen_index, x1, y1, x2, y2, radius, {r, g, b}) Draws a filled rounded rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points using pixel based radius with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_DRAW_POLYGON_usage: grid_gui_draw_polygon(screen_index, {x1, x2, x3 ...}, {y1, y2, y3 ...}, {r, g, b}) Draws a polygon using the x and y coordinate pairs with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_DRAW_POLYGON_FILLED_u: grid_gui_draw_polygon_filled(screen_index, {x1, x2, x3 ...}, {y1, y2, y3 ...}, {r, g, b}) Draws a filled polygon using the x and y coordinate pairs with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_DRAW_TEXT_usage: gui_draw_text(screen_index, 'text', x, y, size, {r, g, b}) Draws the specified text at (x, y) with the specified font size and 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_DRAW_FASTTEXT_usage: gui_draw_fasttext(screen_index, 'text', x, y, size, {r, g, b}) Draws the specified text at (x, y) with the specified font size and 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_DRAW_AREA_FILLED_usag: gui_draw_area_filled(screen_index, x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Fills an area with the specified color, without alpha blending.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_DRAW_DEMO_usage: gui_draw_demo(screen_index, n) Draws the n-th iteration of the demo.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_GUI_GET_RENDER_TIME_usage: gui_get_render_time(screen_index) Returns the time spent rendering between the last two swaps, in microseconds.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_LCD_SET_BACKLIGHT_usage: lcd_set_backlight(strength) Sets the LCD backlight strength between 0 and 255.
GRID_LUA_FNC_L_SCREEN_INDEX_usage: lcd:screen_index() Returns the screen index used by low-level APIs.
GRID_LUA_FNC_L_SCREEN_WIDTH_usage: lcd:screen_width() Returns the screen width in pixels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_L_SCREEN_HEIGHT_usage: lcd:screen_height() Returns the screen height in pixels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_COLOR_CURVE_usage: color_curve(table color_array) Calculates three-point intensity response curve (min, mid, max) from color array. Processes -1 values as current LED colors. Returns three color tables. User overridable.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_COLOR_AUTO_LAYER_usage: color_auto_layer(element self) Calculates LED layer based on current event type. Returns 1 for button/potentiometer events, 2 for encoder events, nil for others. User overridable.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_COLOR_AUTO_VALUE_usage: color_auto_value(element self, int segment_index) Calculates LED intensity value based on element type and state. For endless encoders, returns segment-specific value. For other elements, returns mapped value 0-255. User overridable.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_MIDI_AUTO_CH_usage: midi_auto_ch(element self) Calculates default MIDI channel from grid position. Returns channel 0-15 based on module Y position and page number. User overridable.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_MIDI_AUTO_CMD_usage: midi_auto_cmd(element self) Calculates default MIDI command type. Returns 144 (Note On) for button events, 176 (Control Change) for others. User overridable.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_MIDI_AUTO_P1_usage: midi_auto_p1(element self) Calculates default MIDI parameter 1 (note/CC number) from grid position. Returns 0-127 based on module X position and element index. User overridable.
GRID_LUA_FNC_G_MIDI_AUTO_P2_usage: midi_auto_p2(element self) Calculates default MIDI parameter 2 (value) from element state. Returns value based on event type (button, encoder, potentiometer). User overridable.
GRID_LUA_S_META_init: GRID_LUA_S_TYPE " = { __index = {type = 'system', post_init_cb = function (self) self:"GRID_LUA_FNC_A_INIT_short"() end,"     GRID_LUA_FNC_ASSIGN_META_PAR1_RET("gen", GRID_LUA_FNC_G_ELEMENTNAME_short) ",}}"
GRID_LUA_B_META_init: GRID_LUA_B_TYPE " = { __index = {type = 'button', post_init_cb = function (self) self:"GRID_LUA_FNC_A_INIT_short"() self:"GRID_LUA_FNC_A_BUTTON_short"() end,"     GRID_LUA_FNC_ASSIGN_META_PAR1_RET("gen", GRID_LUA_FNC_G_ELEMENTNAME_short) ","     GRID_LUA_FNC_B_BUTTON_STEP_short " =function (self) local steps, min, max, value = self:" GRID_LUA_FNC_B_BUTTON_MODE_short "(), self:" GRID_LUA_FNC_B_BUTTON_MIN_short "(), self:" GRID_LUA_FNC_B_BUTTON_MAX_short   "(), self:" GRID_LUA_FNC_B_BUTTON_VALUE_short "() if steps == 0 then return false end return value // ((max - min) // steps) end,}}"
GRID_ACTIONSTRING_BUTTON_BUTTON: <?lua --[[@sbc]] self:bmo(0) self:bmi(0) self:bma(127)--[[@sglc]] self:glc(-1,{{-1,-1,-1,1}}) self:glp(-1,-1)--[[@gms]] self:gms(-1,-1,-1,-1) ?>
GRID_LUA_E_META_init: GRID_LUA_E_TYPE " = { __index = { type = 'encoder', post_init_cb = function (self) self:"GRID_LUA_FNC_A_INIT_short"() self:"GRID_LUA_FNC_A_BUTTON_short"() self:"GRID_LUA_FNC_A_ENCODER_short"() end,"     GRID_LUA_FNC_ASSIGN_META_PAR1_RET("gen", GRID_LUA_FNC_G_ELEMENTNAME_short) ","     GRID_LUA_FNC_E_BUTTON_STEP_short " =function (self) local steps, min, max, value = self:" GRID_LUA_FNC_B_BUTTON_MODE_short "(), self:" GRID_LUA_FNC_B_BUTTON_MIN_short "(), self:" GRID_LUA_FNC_B_BUTTON_MAX_short   "(), self:" GRID_LUA_FNC_B_BUTTON_VALUE_short "() if steps == 0 then return false end return value // ((max - min) // steps) end,}}"
GRID_ACTIONSTRING_ENCODER_ENCODER: <?lua --[[@sec]] self:emo(0) self:ev0(50) self:emi(0) self:ema(127) self:ese(100)--[[@sglc]] self:glc(-1,{{-1,-1,-1,1}}) self:glp(-1,-1)--[[@gms]] self:gms(-1,-1,-1,-1) ?>
GRID_LUA_EP_META_init: GRID_LUA_EP_TYPE " = { __index = {type = 'endless', post_init_cb = function (self) self:"GRID_LUA_FNC_A_INIT_short"() self:"GRID_LUA_FNC_A_BUTTON_short"() self:"GRID_LUA_FNC_A_ENDLESS_short"() end,"     GRID_LUA_FNC_ASSIGN_META_PAR1_RET("gen", GRID_LUA_FNC_G_ELEMENTNAME_short) ",}}"
GRID_ACTIONSTRING_ENDLESS_ENDLESS: <?lua --[[@sen]] self:epmo(0) self:epv0(50) self:epmi(0) self:epma(127) self:epse(50)--[[@sglc]] self:glc(-1,{{-1,-1,-1,1}}) self:glp(-1,-1)--[[@gms]] self:gms(-1,-1,-1,-1) ?>
GRID_ACTIONSTRING_ENDLESS_BUTTON: <?lua --[[@sbc]] self:bmo(0) self:bmi(0) self:bma(127)--[[@sglc]] self:glc(-1,{{-1,-1,-1,1}}) self:glp(-1,-1)--[[@gms]] self:gms(-1,-1,-1,-1) ?>
GRID_LUA_P_META_init: GRID_LUA_P_TYPE " = { __index = {type = 'potmeter', post_init_cb = function (self) self:"GRID_LUA_FNC_A_INIT_short"() self:"GRID_LUA_FNC_A_POTMETER_short"() end,"     GRID_LUA_FNC_ASSIGN_META_PAR1_RET("gen", GRID_LUA_FNC_G_ELEMENTNAME_short) ",}}"
GRID_ACTIONSTRING_POTMETER_POTMETER: <?lua --[[@spc]] self:pmo(7) self:pmi(0)  self:pma(127)--[[@sglc]] self:glc(-1,{{-1,-1,-1,1}}) self:glp(-1,-1)--[[@gms]] self:gms(-1,-1,-1,-1) ?>
GRID_LUA_FNC_L_DRAW_SWAP_usage: lcd:draw_swap() Updates the screen with the contents of the background buffer.

GRID_LUA_FNC_L_DRAW_RECTANGLE_usage: lcd:draw_rectangle(x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Draws a rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_L_DRAW_RECTANGLE_FILLED_usa: lcd:draw_rectangle_filled(x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Draws a filled rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_L_DRAW_RECTANGLE_ROUNDED_us: lcd:draw_rectangle_rounded(x1, y1, x2, y2, radius, {r, g, b}) Draws a rounded rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points using pixel based radius with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_L_DRAW_RECTANGLE_ROUNDED_FI: lcd:draw_rectangle_rounded_filled(x1, y1, x2, y2, radius, {r, g, b}) Draws a filled rounded rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points using pixel based radius with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_L_DRAW_POLYGON_usage: lcd:draw_polygon({x1, x2, x3 ...}, {y1, y2, y3 ...}, {r, g, b}) Draws a polygon using the x and y coordinate pairs with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_L_DRAW_POLYGON_FILLED_usage: lcd:draw_polygon_filled({x1, x2, x3 ...}, {y1, y2, y3 ...}, {r, g, b}) Draws a filled polygon using the x and y coordinate pairs with the specified 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_L_DRAW_TEXT_usage: lcd:draw_text('text', x, y, size, {r, g, b}) Draws the specified text at (x, y) with the specified font size and 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_L_DRAW_FASTTEXT_usage: lcd:draw_text_fast('text', x, y, size, {r, g, b}) Draws the specified text at (x, y) with the specified font size and 8-bit color channels.
GRID_LUA_FNC_L_DRAW_AREA_FILLED_usage: lcd:draw_area_filled(x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Fills an area with the specified color, without alpha blending.

GRID_LUA_FNC_L_GET_RENDER_TIME_usage: lcd:get_render_time() Returns the time spent rendering between the last two swaps, in microseconds.

globals.name: calibration_reset
globals.usage: calibration_reset() Deletes the calibration file and initializes all calibrations to their defaults.

globals.name: color_auto_layer
globals.usage: color_auto_layer(element self) Calculates LED layer based on current event type. Returns 1 for button/potentiometer events, 2 for encoder events, nil for others. User overridable.

globals.name: color_auto_value
globals.usage: color_auto_value(element self, int segment_index) Calculates LED intensity value based on element type and state. For endless encoders, returns segment-specific value. For other elements, returns mapped value 0-255. User overridable.

globals.name: color_curve
globals.usage: color_curve(table color_array) Calculates three-point intensity response curve (min, mid, max) from color array. Processes -1 values as current LED colors. Returns three color tables. User overridable.

globals.name: decode
globals.usage: decode(string data) Decodes a serialized protocol value. Returns value (any).

globals.name: element_name
globals.usage: element_name(integer element, string name) Alias helper for element_name_get / element_name_set. Returns name (string).

globals.name: element_name_get
globals.usage: element_name_get(integer element) Gets an element's display name. Returns name (string).

globals.name: element_name_send
globals.usage: element_name_send(integer element) Sends the element name to the editor.

globals.name: element_name_set
globals.usage: element_name_set(integer element, string name) Sets an element's display name.

globals.name: element_count
globals.usage: element_count(void) Returns the number of elements on the current module.

globals.name: event_trigger
globals.usage: event_trigger(integer element, integer event) Triggers an event programmatically.

globals.name: readfile
globals.usage: readfile(string path) Reads a file from the module flash. Returns contents (string).

globals.name: readdir
globals.usage: readdir() Lists directory contents on the module flash. Returns entries (table).

globals.name: gamepad_button_send
globals.usage: gamepad_button_send(integer button, integer state) Sends an HID gamepad button report.

globals.name: gamepad_move_send
globals.usage: gamepad_move_send(integer axis, integer position) Sends an HID gamepad axis report.

globals.name: gui_draw_area_filled
globals.usage: gui_draw_area_filled(screen_index, x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Fills an area with the specified color, without alpha blending.

globals.name: gui_draw_demo
globals.usage: gui_draw_demo(screen_index, n) Draws the n-th iteration of the demo.

globals.name: gui_draw_fasttext
globals.usage: gui_draw_fasttext(screen_index, 'text', x, y, size, {r, g, b}) Draws the specified text at (x, y) with the specified font size and 8-bit color channels.

globals.name: gui_draw_line
globals.usage: grid_gui_draw_line(screen_index, x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Draws a line between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points with the specified 8-bit color channels.

globals.name: gui_draw_pixel
globals.usage: grid_gui_draw_pixel(screen_index, x, y, {r, g, b}) Draws a pixel at (x, y) with the specified 8-bit color channels.

globals.name: gui_draw_polygon
globals.usage: grid_gui_draw_polygon(screen_index, {x1, x2, x3 ...}, {y1, y2, y3 ...}, {r, g, b}) Draws a polygon using the x and y coordinate pairs with the specified 8-bit color channels.

globals.name: gui_draw_polygon_filled
globals.usage: grid_gui_draw_polygon_filled(screen_index, {x1, x2, x3 ...}, {y1, y2, y3 ...}, {r, g, b}) Draws a filled polygon using the x and y coordinate pairs with the specified 8-bit color channels.

globals.name: gui_draw_rectangle
globals.usage: grid_gui_draw_rectangle(screen_index, x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Draws a rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points with the specified 8-bit color channels.

globals.name: gui_draw_rectangle_filled
globals.usage: grid_gui_draw_rectangle_filled(screen_index, x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Draws a filled rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points with the specified 8-bit color channels.

globals.name: gui_draw_rectangle_rounded
globals.usage: grid_gui_draw_rectangle_rounded(screen_index, x1, y1, x2, y2, radius, {r, g, b}) Draws a rounded rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points using pixel based radius with the specified 8-bit color channels.

globals.name: gui_draw_rectangle_rounded_filled
globals.usage: grid_gui_draw_rectangle_rounded_filled(screen_index, x1, y1, x2, y2, radius, {r, g, b}) Draws a filled rounded rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points using pixel based radius with the specified 8-bit color channels.

globals.name: gui_draw_swap
globals.usage: grid_gui_draw_swap(screen_index) Updates the screen with the contents of the background buffer.

globals.name: gui_draw_text
globals.usage: gui_draw_text(screen_index, 'text', x, y, size, {r, g, b}) Draws the specified text at (x, y) with the specified font size and 8-bit color channels.

globals.name: gui_get_render_time
globals.usage: gui_get_render_time(screen_index) Returns the time spent rendering between the last two swaps, in microseconds.

globals.name: hardware_configuration
globals.usage: hardware_configuration() Returns the hardware configuration ID. Returns hwcfg (integer).

globals.name: immediate_send
globals.usage: immediate_send(int x, int y, string lua_code) Executes lua_code on the module addressed by x and y. Use x = nil and y = nil to trigger on all modules.

globals.name: keyboard_send
globals.usage: keyboard_send() Sends an HID keyboard report.

globals.name: lcd_set_backlight
globals.usage: lcd_set_backlight(strength) Sets the LCD backlight strength between 0 and 255.

globals.name: led_address_get
globals.usage: led_address_get(int element, int n) Returns the hardware index of an element's n-th led, or nil if either parameter is out of bounds.

globals.name: led_default_blue
globals.usage: led_default_blue(integer value) Gets or sets the default blue component of the LED. Returns blue Current value (integer).

globals.name: led_color
globals.usage: led_color(integer layer, table colors) Sets or gets the LED color (composite helper).

globals.name: led_animation_rate
globals.usage: led_animation_rate(integer value) Gets or sets the LED animation rate. Returns rate (integer).

globals.name: led_default_green
globals.usage: led_default_green(integer value) Gets or sets the default green component of the LED. Returns green Current value (integer).

globals.name: led_color_max
globals.usage: led_color_max(integer value) Gets or sets the LED color maximum intensity. Returns max Current maximum (integer).

globals.name: led_color_mid
globals.usage: led_color_mid(integer value) Gets or sets the LED color mid intensity. Returns mid Current mid (integer).

globals.name: led_color_min
globals.usage: led_color_min(integer value) Gets or sets the LED color minimum intensity. Returns min Current minimum (integer).

globals.name: led_value
globals.usage: led_value(integer value) Gets or sets the LED phase value. Returns phase Current value (integer).

globals.name: led_animation_phase_rate_type
globals.usage: led_animation_phase_rate_type(integer phase, integer frequency, integer shape) Composite phase/frequency/shape setter.

globals.name: led_default_red
globals.usage: led_default_red(integer value) Gets or sets the default red component of the LED. Returns red Current value (integer).

globals.name: led_animation_type
globals.usage: led_animation_type(integer value) Gets or sets the LED animation type / shape. Returns shape (integer).

globals.name: led_timeout
globals.usage: led_timeout(integer value) Gets or sets the LED timeout. Returns timeout Current timeout (integer).

globals.name: limit
globals.usage: limit(number value, number min, number max) Clamps *value* between *min* and *max*. Returns clamped (number).

globals.name: lookup
globals.usage: lookup(string key) Looks up a protocol value by key. Returns value (any).

globals.name: map_saturate
globals.usage: map_saturate(number value, number from_min, number from_max, number to_min, number to_max) Linearly maps *value* from [from_min, from_max] to [to_min, to_max] and saturates the result to [to_min, to_max]. Returns mapped Mapped and clamped result (number).

globals.name: midirx_enabled
globals.usage: midirx_enabled(integer value) Gets or sets the MIDI-RX enabled flag. Returns enabled (integer).

globals.name: midirx_sync
globals.usage: midirx_sync(integer value) Gets or sets the MIDI-RX sync flag. Returns sync (integer).

globals.name: midi_sysex_send
globals.usage: midi_sysex_send() Sends a MIDI SysEx message.

globals.name: midi_auto_ch
globals.usage: midi_auto_ch(element self) Calculates default MIDI channel from grid position. Returns channel 0-15 based on module Y position and page number. User overridable.

globals.name: midi_auto_cmd
globals.usage: midi_auto_cmd(element self) Calculates default MIDI command type. Returns 144 (Note On) for button events, 176 (Control Change) for others. User overridable.

globals.name: midi_auto_p1
globals.usage: midi_auto_p1(element self) Calculates default MIDI parameter 1 (note/CC number) from grid position. Returns 0-127 based on module X position and element index. User overridable.

globals.name: midi_auto_p2
globals.usage: midi_auto_p2(element self) Calculates default MIDI parameter 2 (value) from element state. Returns value based on event type (button, encoder, potentiometer). User overridable.

globals.name: midi_send
globals.usage: midi_send(int channel, int command, int parameter1, int parameter2) Sends standard MIDI message

globals.name: module_position_x
globals.usage: module_position_x() Returns x Module X position on the grid (integer).

globals.name: module_position_y
globals.usage: module_position_y() Returns y Module Y position on the grid (integer).

globals.name: module_rotation
globals.usage: module_rotation() Returns rotation Module rotation (0, 1, 2, 3) (integer).

globals.name: mouse_button_send
globals.usage: mouse_button_send(integer button, integer state) Sends an HID mouse-button report.

globals.name: mouse_move_send
globals.usage: mouse_move_send(integer axis, integer position) Sends an HID mouse-move report.

globals.name: package_send
globals.usage: package_send(string message) Sends a package to the editor.

globals.name: page_current
globals.usage: page_current() Returns the currently active page number. Returns page (integer).

globals.name: page_load
globals.usage: page_load(integer page) Loads a specific page.

globals.name: page_next
globals.usage: page_next() Switch to the next page.

globals.name: page_previous
globals.usage: page_previous() Switch to the previous page.

globals.name: potmeter_calibration_get
globals.usage: potmeter_calibration_get() Returns raw potentiometer values as an array of integers.

globals.name: potmeter_center_set
globals.usage: potmeter_center_set({ int c1, ... }) Sets potentiometer calibration centers from an array of integers.

globals.name: potmeter_detent_set
globals.usage: potmeter_detent_set({ int c1, ... }, bool high) Sets potentiometer detent bounds from an array of integers, where high = true sets high bounds and high = false sets low bounds.

globals.name: random8
globals.usage: random8() Returns a pseudo-random integer in [0, 255]. Returns value (integer).

globals.name: range_calibration_get
globals.usage: range_calibration_get() Returns raw minimum and maximum values as an array of integer arrays.

globals.name: range_calibration_set
globals.usage: range_calibration_set({ { int min1, int max1 }, ... }) Sets minimum and maximum values from an array of integer arrays.

globals.name: segment_calculate
globals.usage: segment_calculate(integer value, integer min, integer max, integer segment_index, integer segment_count) Calculates the intensity value for a single LED segment when visualising an endless-encoder position. Returns intensity Segment brightness (0–255) (integer).

globals.name: sign
globals.usage: sign(number value) Returns the sign of a number: -1, 0, or 1. Returns sign (integer).

globals.name: string_get
globals.usage: string_get(string key) Gets a stored string value. Returns value (string).

globals.name: timer_source
globals.usage: timer_source() Returns the timer event source element. Returns element (integer).

globals.name: timer_start
globals.usage: timer_start(integer element, integer period) Starts the element timer.

globals.name: timer_stop
globals.usage: timer_stop(integer element) Stops the element timer.

globals.name: version_major
globals.usage: version_major() Returns major Firmware major version (integer).

globals.name: version_minor
globals.usage: version_minor() Returns minor Firmware minor version (integer).

globals.name: version_patch
globals.usage: version_patch() Returns patch Firmware patch version (integer).

globals.name: websocket_send
globals.usage: websocket_send(string message) Sends a string via WebSocket.

button.name: button_elapsed_time
button.usage: self:button_elapsed_time() Returns the elapsed time since last button event in ms. Returns ms (integer).

button.name: button_max
button.usage: self:button_max(integer value) Gets or sets the button maximum. Returns max Current maximum (integer).

button.name: button_min
button.usage: self:button_min(integer value) Gets or sets the button minimum. Returns min Current minimum (integer).

button.name: button_mode
button.usage: self:button_mode(integer value) Gets or sets the button mode (step count). 0 = momentary. Returns mode Current mode (integer).

button.name: button_state
button.usage: self:button_state() Returns the button state (0 = released, 127 = pressed). Returns state (integer).

button.name: button_step
button.usage: self:button_step() Returns the current step index, or false if mode is 0. Returns step (integer|false).

button.name: button_value
button.usage: self:button_value(integer value) Gets or sets the button value. Returns value Current button value (integer).

button.name: element_index
button.usage: self:element_index() Returns the zero-based element index. Returns index (integer).

button.name: led_index
button.usage: self:led_index() Returns the hardware LED index for this element. Returns led_index (integer).

lcd.name: draw_area_filled
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_area_filled(x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Fills an area with the specified color, without alpha blending.

lcd.name: draw_demo
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_demo(n) Draws the n-th iteration of the demo.

lcd.name: draw_text_fast
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_text_fast('text', x, y, size, {r, g, b}) Draws the specified text at (x, y) with the specified font size and 8-bit color channels.

lcd.name: draw_line
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_line(x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Draws a line between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points with the specified 8-bit color channels.

lcd.name: draw_pixel
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_pixel(x, y, {r, g, b}) Draws a pixel at (x, y) with the specified 8-bit color channels.

lcd.name: draw_polygon
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_polygon({x1, x2, x3 ...}, {y1, y2, y3 ...}, {r, g, b}) Draws a polygon using the x and y coordinate pairs with the specified 8-bit color channels.

lcd.name: draw_polygon_filled
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_polygon_filled({x1, x2, x3 ...}, {y1, y2, y3 ...}, {r, g, b}) Draws a filled polygon using the x and y coordinate pairs with the specified 8-bit color channels.

lcd.name: draw_rectangle
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_rectangle(x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Draws a rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points with the specified 8-bit color channels.

lcd.name: draw_rectangle_filled
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_rectangle_filled(x1, y1, x2, y2, {r, g, b}) Draws a filled rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points with the specified 8-bit color channels.

lcd.name: draw_rectangle_rounded
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_rectangle_rounded(x1, y1, x2, y2, radius, {r, g, b}) Draws a rounded rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points using pixel based radius with the specified 8-bit color channels.

lcd.name: draw_rectangle_rounded_filled
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_rectangle_rounded_filled(x1, y1, x2, y2, radius, {r, g, b}) Draws a filled rounded rectangle between (x1, y1) and (x2, y2) points using pixel based radius with the specified 8-bit color channels.

lcd.name: draw_swap
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_swap() Updates the screen with the contents of the background buffer.

lcd.name: draw_text
lcd.usage: lcd:draw_text('text', x, y, size, {r, g, b}) Draws the specified text at (x, y) with the specified font size and 8-bit color channels.

lcd.name: element_index
lcd.usage: self:element_index() Returns the zero-based element index. Returns index (integer).

lcd.name: get_render_time
lcd.usage: lcd:get_render_time() Returns the time spent rendering between the last two swaps, in microseconds.

lcd.name: screen_height
lcd.usage: lcd:screen_height() Returns the screen height in pixels.

lcd.name: screen_index
lcd.usage: lcd:screen_index() Returns the screen index used by low-level APIs.

lcd.name: screen_width
lcd.usage: lcd:screen_width() Returns the screen width in pixels.
